title: SSE event endpoint
analysis:
The task requires implementing a Server-Sent Events endpoint at GET /api/events that polls the pipeline_events table and pushes real-time updates to the browser.

Existing infrastructure:
- autopilot/db.py has CatalogDB with pipeline_events CRUD: insert_event(event_type, stage, job_id, payload_json) -> int, get_events_since(event_id) -> list[dict], prune_events(hours=24)
- pipeline_events schema: event_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, stage TEXT, job_id TEXT, payload_json TEXT, created_at TEXT DEFAULT datetime('now')
- autopilot/web/app.py has create_app(db_path) factory storing db_path in app.state.db_path
- No existing routers — this will be the first APIRouter registered via app.include_router()
- tests/conftest.py has catalog_db fixture (in-memory CatalogDB with autocommit)
- tests/test_web_app.py shows the pattern: app fixture creates FastAPI via create_app(tmp_path), client fixture wraps with Starlette TestClient
- Client-side app.js already has connectSSE('/api/events') with EventSource and notification listener expecting event types
- sse-starlette 3.3.2 is installed in the env (required by mcp) but NOT yet in pyproject.toml

Approach:
1. Add sse-starlette to pyproject.toml Web dependencies
2. Create autopilot/web/routes/sse.py with APIRouter containing GET /api/events
3. Create get_db FastAPI Depends function to construct CatalogDB from app.state.db_path
4. Implement async generator that polls get_events_since() every 1s, yields ServerSentEvent objects
5. Use sse-starlette's EventSourceResponse with ping=15 for keepalive (matches 15-second requirement)
6. Support Last-Event-ID header for reconnection
7. Prune events periodically (~every 60 polls) rather than every poll
8. Register the router in app.py

Testing strategy: The SSE endpoint is a streaming response. For tests, we'll insert events into a real in-memory CatalogDB before connecting, then read from the streaming TestClient response. To prevent tests from hanging (the generator is infinite), we'll either use a timeout or inject a mechanism to break the loop after consuming expected events. The pragmatic approach is to use httpx's stream context and read initial events, then close.

prerequisites.description: Add 'sse-starlette' to pyproject.toml in the Web dependencies section alongside fastapi/uvicorn/jinja2

steps.description: Write tests/test_sse.py with fixtures: (1) sse_db fixture that creates an in-memory CatalogDB with autocommit, (2) sse_app fixture that calls create_app with a tmp_path db but then overrides app.state.db_path to point to the in-memory db file or patches the dependency, (3) sse_client fixture with Starlette TestClient. Write TestSSEEndpointBasic class with test_sse_endpoint_returns_200_event_stream — GET /api/events returns 200 with content-type text/event-stream.

Create autopilot/web/routes/sse.py with: (1) APIRouter with prefix '', (2) get_db dependency that creates CatalogDB from request.app.state.db_path, (3) GET /api/events endpoint that returns EventSourceResponse from a minimal async generator yielding nothing (just awaits forever with asyncio.sleep). Update autopilot/web/routes/__init__.py to export the router. Register SSE router in app.py via app.include_router(sse_router). Make the basic 200 + content-type test pass.

Write TestSSEEventDelivery class: (1) test_events_delivered_in_order — insert 3 events via CatalogDB (stage_started, job_started, stage_completed), connect to SSE endpoint, verify all 3 events arrive in correct order; (2) test_event_has_correct_sse_fields — verify each SSE event has id field (event_id), event field (event_type), and data field (JSON with event_type, stage, job_id); (3) test_event_data_payload_structure — verify the JSON data payload contains event_type, stage, job_id, and original payload_json data.

Implement the full async event generator in sse.py: poll db.get_events_since(last_event_id) every 1 second using asyncio.sleep(1). For each event, yield a ServerSentEvent with id=str(event_id), event=event_type, data=json.dumps({event_type, stage, job_id, ...payload_json parsed}). Track last_event_id across polls. Make event delivery tests pass.

Write TestSSEReconnection class: (1) test_last_event_id_header_resumes_from_position — insert 5 events, connect with Last-Event-ID: 3 header, verify only events 4 and 5 are received; (2) test_missing_last_event_id_starts_from_zero — connect without header, verify all events are received; (3) test_invalid_last_event_id_starts_from_zero — connect with Last-Event-ID: 'abc' (non-numeric), verify all events are received.
Implement Last-Event-ID support: read request.headers.get('last-event-id', '0'), parse as int with fallback to 0, use as initial last_event_id for get_events_since(). Make reconnection tests pass.

Write TestSSEPruning class: (1) test_old_events_pruned_after_polls — insert a backdated event (>24h old via raw SQL) and a recent event, trigger SSE endpoint for enough polls, verify the old event was deleted from the DB; (2) test_recent_events_not_pruned — insert only recent events, after polling, verify all events still exist in DB.

Add periodic pruning to the async generator: maintain a poll_count counter, call db.prune_events(hours=24) when poll_count % 60 == 0 (approximately once per minute). Wrap in try/except to avoid killing the SSE stream on pruning errors. Make pruning tests pass.

Write TestSSEEventTypes class: (1) test_all_event_types_forwarded — insert one event for each of the 11 event types (stage_started, stage_completed, stage_error, job_started, job_completed, job_error, job_progress, gate_waiting, gate_approved, gate_skipped, run_completed, run_failed), connect to SSE, verify each event type is received with correct 'event' field. (2) test_valid_event_types_constant_defined — verify VALID_EVENT_TYPES constant exists and contains all 11 types.

Define VALID_EVENT_TYPES tuple constant in sse.py with all 11 event types for documentation. Ensure event_type from DB is passed through directly as the SSE 'event' field. Run full test suite to verify all tests pass. Final cleanup: ensure proper DB connection cleanup on generator exit (try/finally), consistent error handling.

design_decisions.decision: Use sse-starlette package rather than manual StreamingResponse
design_decisions.rationale: sse-starlette 3.3.2 is already installed (required by mcp), provides clean ServerSentEvent/EventSourceResponse abstractions, built-in ping/keepalive support (ping=15), and proper SSE protocol handling including cache-control headers. The task description suggests this approach.

design_decisions.decision: Use EventSourceResponse(ping=15) for keepalive instead of manual keepalive comments
design_decisions.rationale: sse-starlette's EventSourceResponse has a built-in ping parameter that sends keepalive comments every N seconds. Setting ping=15 matches the task requirement of 15-second keepalives without manual timer management in the generator.

design_decisions.decision: Create a get_db FastAPI dependency that creates CatalogDB per SSE connection
design_decisions.rationale: app.py stores db_path in app.state. Since CatalogDB uses check_same_thread=False and WAL mode, creating a connection per SSE stream is safe and avoids sharing a connection across concurrent streams. The connection is closed when the generator exits via try/finally.

design_decisions.decision: Prune events every ~60 poll cycles rather than every poll
design_decisions.rationale: Running DELETE on every 1-second poll adds unnecessary DB write overhead. Pruning once per minute is sufficient for 24-hour-old data cleanup. A simple counter modulo check achieves this without a separate timer or background task.

design_decisions.decision: Use APIRouter for the SSE routes — establishing the first router pattern in this codebase
design_decisions.rationale: The app currently has no routers (only an inline health check). Using APIRouter establishes the extensible pattern for future route modules (dashboard, media, gates, etc.) and follows FastAPI best practices for route organization.

design_decisions.decision: Parse payload_json from DB into the SSE data dict rather than nesting as a string
design_decisions.rationale: The SSE data payload should be a flat(ish) JSON object for easy HTMX consumption. payload_json from the DB is already a JSON string, so we parse it and merge into the data dict alongside event_type/stage/job_id, providing a clean API to the browser.

design_decisions.decision: Test SSE using a real in-memory SQLite DB with pre-inserted events, read first batch then close
design_decisions.rationale: SSE endpoints are infinite streaming responses. For testing, we pre-insert events so they're available on first poll, then read the initial batch from the TestClient stream and close. This avoids hanging tests while verifying real DB-to-SSE integration.

reuse.how: Core polling method — returns events with event_id > given id, ordered ascending. Used directly in the SSE async generator on each 1-second poll cycle.
Existing pruning method — deletes events older than N hours. Called every ~60 polls in the SSE generator for periodic cleanup.
Used in tests to seed the pipeline_events table with test data before connecting to the SSE endpoint.
SSE route dependency reads request.app.state.db_path to create a CatalogDB connection for the streaming response.
Reuse the in-memory CatalogDB fixture pattern for pre-populating events in SSE tests.
Follow the same create_app(tmp_path) + TestClient pattern for SSE tests, with added DB setup for event data.
EventSourceResponse wraps the async generator with proper SSE headers and keepalive. ServerSentEvent formats individual events with id/event/data fields.
